title: Repository Changelog
format: Keep a Changelog 1.1.0 conventions, expressed as JSON.

entries.author: AquaVision founding team (drafted with AI assistance)
entries.summary: Added assumed five-year financial projection (pessimistic / base / optimistic) to financial-projections.json with subscriber counts, annual revenue, opex, and net burn — all tagged modeled_projection or assumption. Added chart-assumed-revenue-projection.json. traction.json remains honestly empty.
added: financial-projections.json#/assumed_financial_projection — three-scenario 2026–2030 model tied to unit-economics.json inputs.
Phase spend envelope USD bands on financial-projections.json#/phase_spend_plan.
chart-assumed-revenue-projection.json — line chart of assumed annual revenue by scenario.
changed: financial-projections.json READ_THIS_FIRST and revenue_scenario_structure status to reflect assumed numbers exist but are not measured.
decisions_recorded: Assumed subscriber and revenue figures live only in financial-projections.json with explicit provenance — not copied into traction.json, which stays empty until earned.

entries.author: AquaVision founding team (drafted with AI assistance)
entries.summary: Repositioned the repository narrative from a hardware-first drone company to a software-first, three-phase aquaculture platform: Phase 1 free app (acquisition) -> Phase 2 premium tier and marketplace (monetization) -> Phase 3 IoT sensors and OmniDrone (smart aquaculture, enterprise). The Phase 3 content is the same hardware engineering programme the repository already contained; it is reframed as an upgrade layer rather than the company's core product. No ids, provenance tags, or real data were changed — this is a narrative and framing rewrite, and traction.json remains honestly empty.
added: Phase-based framing across company-profile.json, milestones-roadmap.json, team-structure.json, and brand-identity.json.
Two new product-line entries: AquaVision App (Phase 1) and AquaVision Premium (Phase 2), alongside the existing OmniDrone/OmniDock/PondTwin/Platform entries now labeled by phase.
New glossary terms: Phase 1 (Foundation), Phase 2 (Monetization), Phase 3 (Smart Aquaculture), Freemium, PondTwin.
Two new team roles: Head of Product (app & premium tier) and Growth & Community Marketing Lead, reflecting the Phase 1 acquisition focus.
known_gaps: This pass covered data/00_meta and data/01_company only. Other sections (market sizing, business model, financials, go-to-market, hardware/software detail files) still read as hardware-first as of this entry and need the same phased reframing for the repository to be fully internally consistent.
No real user, farmer, or financial data exists for any phase. Every Phase 1/2 metric in the roadmap is a design target or illustrative planning figure, tagged as such, not a measured result.
decisions_recorded: Chose to keep all four original product ids (omnidrone, omnidock, pondtwin, aquavision-platform) unchanged and add two new ids (aquavision-app, aquavision-premium) rather than renumber anything, since ids are treated as permanent keys elsewhere in the repository.

Chose to nest the original six-stage hardware development plan (TRL 2 through 8) inside Phase 3 as sub-stages, rather than delete or compress it, because that engineering rigor is still valid — only its position in the company's story changed.

Chose not to invent specific user, revenue, or conversion numbers for Phase 1/2 milestones; used order-of-magnitude illustrative targets tagged design_target instead, consistent with the existing rule that traction.json stays honestly empty until real.

entries.author: AquaVision founding team (drafted with AI assistance)
entries.summary: Initial construction of the OmniDrone knowledge repository from the founding concept description.
added: Full 14-section repository skeleton with manifest-driven index for webpage consumption.
Schema registry and data dictionary establishing the provenance discipline used throughout.
Problem space: taxonomy, stakeholder pain points, evidenced incident case studies, cost-of-inaction model.
Market: industry statistics sourced to FAO SOFIA 2024, TAM/SAM/SOM model, competitor teardown.
Hardware: airframe, probe winch, sensor payload, carriage/ejection, power budget, dock, indicative BOM.
Software: architecture, pipeline, model cards, digital twin specification, telemetry schema, API, dashboard spec.
Research corpus: thematic study collections across eight themes plus consolidated bibliography.
Operations, compliance, business model, impact, chart-ready datasets, and pitch/thesis kit.
known_gaps: No field trial data exists. Every performance figure is a design target or a projection, tagged as such.

Bibliography entries derived from search-engine summaries carry verification_status = cited_from_search_result and MUST be opened and checked before thesis submission. Do not submit a reference list you have not read.
Financial projections are unvalidated models built on stated assumptions. The assumptions are listed so they can be attacked; attack them.
Regulatory analysis reflects a reading of publicly summarised CAAP rules as of mid-2026 and is not legal advice. Confirm with CAAP directly before any BVLOS commitment appears in an investor deck.
Competitor pricing is largely absent from public sources and is marked unknown rather than guessed.
decisions_recorded: Chose to leave traction.json deliberately empty rather than populate it with invented customers. A pitch with honest zeros and a credible plan survives diligence; a pitch with invented traction does not.
Chose in-situ probe + multispectral fusion as the core scientific claim, rather than imagery alone, because uncalibrated indices do not produce defensible time series.
Chose to treat biosecurity cross-contamination as a first-class hardware requirement rather than an operational footnote, because it is the strongest technical objection to the multi-site concept.

planned.intent: Populate water-quality-standards.json with the verbatim DENR and BFAR numerical criteria after obtaining the primary administrative orders. Current entries are placeholders pending primary-source retrieval.
Replace competitor entries with findings from direct vendor quotes and demo access rather than public marketing copy.
First real data release: replace illustrative_synthetic telemetry with a genuine sortie dataset from the pilot site, and populate model performance fields from actual evaluation runs.

maintenance_policy.on_edit: Bump the file's own `version` and `last_updated`, and add an entry here. A repository whose changelog lags its content is a repository nobody trusts.
maintenance_policy.on_new_claim: Any new quantitative claim requires a provenance tag and, if verified_external, a bibliography entry. No exceptions — this is the rule that keeps the thesis defensible.
maintenance_policy.on_data_arrival: When real data replaces synthetic data, remove the illustrative_synthetic tag in the same commit. A stale synthetic tag on real data is nearly as damaging as the reverse.
